stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Many soil microbes break down organic matter into compounds plants can absorb.
  • This process is called nutrient cycling.
  • Researchers use nutrient cycling rates to assess soil health in farmland.
  • Soil ecologist Mariana Costa’s team measured nutrient cycling in coffee plantations in the highlands of Costa Rica to evaluate how shade-tree planting affects soil health.
  • The study found that plots with shade trees had 38% higher nutrient cycling rates than open plots.
stem: The student wants to present the study to an audience already familiar with nutrient cycling. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Researchers found that nutrient cycling—the process by which microbes break down organic matter into compounds plants can absorb—was 38% higher in shaded coffee plots.
Measuring nutrient cycling rates is a common way for researchers to assess the health of farmland soil.
Mariana Costa’s team studied nutrient cycling (the breakdown of organic matter by soil microbes) in coffee plantations in Costa Rica’s highlands.
Mariana Costa’s team measured nutrient cycling in coffee plantations in the highlands of Costa Rica to evaluate how shade-tree planting affects soil health.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. This choice presents the study in a way that assumes the audience is already familiar with nutrient cycling.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice isn’t suited for an audience already familiar with nutrient cycling. A familiar audience wouldn’t need to have the term defined. Choice B is incorrect. This choice doesn’t present the study. It only states a general fact. Choice C is incorrect. This choice isn’t suited for an audience already familiar with nutrient cycling. A familiar audience wouldn’t need to have the term defined.
